Politics.headline: Commission welcomes political agreement for a broader European Globalisation Adjustment Fund supporting workers
Politics.detail: The European Parliament and EU Member States have reached a political agreement to strengthen the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund for Displaced Workers (EGF), enabling earlier assistance for at-risk workers and broadening support for those facing job loss due to restructuring, including upskilling within companies.

Politics.headline: Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on February 26, 2026
Politics.detail: Mao Ning stated that China advocates resolving issues through political and diplomatic channels and opposes the use or threat of force in international relations when asked about potential US aggression towards Iran and whether China would support Iran alongside Russia. China supports the Iranian government and people in upholding their nation's stability and legitimate rights and interests and hopes various parties will cherish peace, exercise restraint, and resolve differences through dialogue.
Politics.source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China

Politics.headline: No clear frontrunner for governor, but new poll names five with the best shot
Politics.detail: A new poll by the Public Policy Institute of California reveals no clear frontrunner in the California governor's race, dividing the candidates into five with support ranging from 10% to 14% and six who are in the single digits. The poll, which was conducted in early February, may not fully reflect the potential of San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan, who recently entered the race with significant financial backing.

Politics.headline: Thune declares 'talking filibuster' dead
Politics.detail: Senate Majority Leader John Thune is facing relentless pressure from all directions as he navigates a politically perilous fight over the SAVE America Act.

Politics.headline: Trump says lawmakers Ilhan Omar, Rashida Tlaib should be removed from US
Politics.detail: US President Donald Trump lashed out at two congresswomen after their protests during his State of the Union address.

Finance & Economy.headline: EBRD Expects Growth in its Regions to Pick Up
Finance & Economy.detail: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) forecasts growth across its regions to increase from an estimated 3.4 per cent in 2025 to 3.6 per cent in 2026 and 3.7 per cent in 2027, according to its latest Regional Economic Prospects report. Growth is further sustained by continued disinflation, which is supporting consumption, and large infrastructure projects, which are boosting investment.

Finance & Economy.headline: Ocado to cut 1,000 jobs in £150m cost-saving drive
Finance & Economy.detail: The online grocer Ocado plans to cut 1,000 jobs in an effort to save £150 million. This move comes as the company seeks to streamline operations and reduce costs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Nvidia and other tech stocks lead Wall Street higher
Finance & Economy.detail: Boosted by Nvidia's earnings report, US stocks are rising, signaling a recovery from AI-induced fears. The report helped ease some investor fears over the boom in artificial intelligence.

Finance & Economy.headline: Australia union seeks urgent talks with WiseTech over AI‑driven job cuts.
Finance & Economy.detail: An Australian union is seeking urgent talks with WiseTech regarding potential job cuts driven by the implementation of AI technologies. The union aims to understand the scope and impact of these AI-driven changes on the workforce.

Finance & Economy.headline: DGCA mandates full air ticket refunds, zero fees for cancellations within 48 hrs
Finance & Economy.detail: India's Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has mandated that airlines provide full ticket refunds and waive cancellation fees for flights cancelled within 48 hours of booking. This new rule aims to protect consumer interests and provide more flexibility for travelers.

Technology.headline: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked 2026 recap: All the news on Galaxy S26 Ultra, Privacy Display, Buds 4 Pro
Technology.detail: Samsung's Unpacked event showcased the new Galaxy S26 Ultra, a privacy display feature, and the Buds 4 Pro.

Technology.headline: How to turn on Private DNS Mode on Android - and why it's a must for security
Technology.detail: A guide on enabling Private DNS mode on Android devices to enhance online security and privacy.

Technology.headline: I Got to Try Samsung's Galaxy S26 Phones: I'm Seriously Impressed With This New Feature
Technology.detail: Hands-on experience with Samsung's Galaxy S26 phones reveals impressive new features.

Technology.headline: Nvidia Warns of 'Very Tight' GPU Supplies for at Least Next 2 Quarters
Technology.detail: Nvidia is warning that GPU supplies will be very tight for at least the next two quarters.

Technology.headline: Your car's tire sensors could be used to track you
Technology.detail: The technology in your car's tire sensors could potentially be used to track your movements.

Science.headline: Avoiding meat might slightly lower the odds of reaching 100 — but only for frail, underweight seniors
Science.detail: In very old age, staying strong and maintaining muscle matters more than long-term disease prevention, as indicated by research that found that older adults who included fish, eggs, or dairy in their diets lived longer.

Science.headline: Shingles shot might slow down the aging process
Science.detail: A study of over 3,800 Americans aged 70 and older revealed that those who received the shingles vaccine exhibited a slower rate of functional decline, suggesting the vaccine may have benefits beyond preventing shingles.

Science.headline: Microplastics detected in prostate cancer tumors
Science.detail: A new study has found microplastics in nearly all prostate cancer tumors examined, with tumor tissue containing approximately 2.5 times more plastic than nearby healthy tissue, suggesting a potential link between microplastic exposure and cancer.

Science.headline: Early Humans May Have Invented System of Symbols Long Before Writing
Science.detail: New research suggests early humans may have used a system of symbols long before the development of writing, indicating a more advanced cognitive ability than previously thought.

Science.headline: Astrophysicists Propose New Method to Measure Hubble Constant
Science.detail: Astrophysicists are exploring a novel approach to measure the Hubble Constant, potentially resolving the ongoing debate about the expansion rate of the universe.

Health.headline: Virginia expands maternal mental health and cervical cancer screening efforts
Health.detail: New initiatives in Virginia aim to improve access to women's health services, particularly in rural and underserved communities, and prohibit insurers from imposing prior authorization requirements or step therapy protocols for prescription medications used to treat maternal mental health conditions. The University of Virginia also launched a new initiative to expand cervical cancer screenings in Southwest Virginia using AI-powered digital colposcopy tools.

Health.headline: Tobacco crisis: WHO European Region projected to remain worst globally by 2030
Health.detail: The WHO European Region is projected to remain the worst globally for tobacco use by 2030, with concerning trends among women and young people.  Adolescents in the Region have the highest average e-cigarette use prevalence in the world, especially girls aged 13–15.

Health.headline: NC lawmakers wrestle with broken mental health system, ask public for input
Health.detail: North Carolina lawmakers are grappling with problems in the state's mental health and criminal justice systems, aiming to address the cycle of people with mental illness moving between emergency departments, jail cells, and psychiatric hospitals. State-run psychiatric hospitals are filled with patients arrested for crimes who aren't mentally well enough to participate in their legal cases.

Health.headline: New GLP-1 pill helps patients lose up to 8% of body weight, trial shows
Health.detail: A new GLP-1 pill, Orforglipron, led to greater weight loss than semaglutide tablets in a trial, potentially offering a more effective oral alternative to injections. The trial demonstrated that patients lost up to 8% of their body weight using the new pill.

Health.headline: Health chief Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is on tour to tout his MAHA food initiatives and drug pricing over vaccines, an issue GOP pollsters see as a liability
Health.detail: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is promoting his Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) food initiatives and drug pricing efforts, while downplaying vaccines, as some Republicans worry that vaccine policies could be a political weakness in the midterm elections. The administration is reportedly shifting its focus to food and drug pricing, areas seen as more broadly popular than vaccine-related issues.

Sports.headline: T20 World Cup: Aiden Markram's 82, Lungi Ngidi's 3/30 help South Africa thrash West Indies and put ball in India's court
Sports.detail: Aiden Markram and Lungi Ngidi led South Africa to a dominant victory over the West Indies in the T20 World Cup, increasing the pressure on India in the tournament. South Africa's win positions them strongly in Group 1.

Sports.headline: India XI vs Zimbabwe: Rinku Singh, Washington Sundar Out; 2 Big Changes Made
Sports.detail: India made two significant changes to their lineup for the T20 World Cup match against Zimbabwe, with Rinku Singh and Washington Sundar excluded from the starting XI. The team is aiming to address top-order concerns.

Sports.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo buys stake in Saudi-owned second-tier Spanish club
Sports.detail: Cristiano Ronaldo has expanded his football interests by purchasing a stake in Almería, a second-tier Spanish club owned by Saudi Arabian interests. This move marks Ronaldo's further involvement in football beyond playing.

Sports.headline: 'It's always unfair': Gutho in passionate defence of Dragons teammate... and Bulldogs rival
Sports.detail: Clint Gutherson defends Kyle Flanagan and Lachlan Galvin ahead of Las Vegas games, saying the media scrutiny is unfair and that Flanagan was one of the Dragons' most consistent players last year. He also mentioned it was cool to spend much of this week training out of the UFC Performance Institute.

Sports.headline: MEDIA DAY DISPATCH: the class of 2026 fire up on Thursday at Buriram
Sports.detail: MotoGP is back in action at Buriram with the PT Grand Prix of Thailand, as riders share their thoughts and address milestones and testing surprises. Reigning World Champion Marc Marquez is working to catch up after recovering from stomach issues during testing.

Entertainment.headline: Korean director Park Chan-wook to lead Cannes jury
Entertainment.detail: Award-winning Korean director Park Chan-wook will preside over the jury of the 79th Cannes Film Festival.

Entertainment.headline: Rock band The Beach Boys to celebrate 60 years of 'Pet Sounds' in Dubai
Entertainment.detail: Marking more than half a century of making music, American rock band The Beach Boys will celebrate the 60th anniversary of their renowned album 'Pet Sounds' at the Coca-Cola Arena in Dubai on June 11.

Entertainment.headline: 'Scream 7': Matthew Lillard Recreates His 'Most Embarrassing Ever' Red Carpet Look (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: Matthew Lillard hilariously recreates his most embarrassing red carpet look in an exclusive interview about 'Scream 7'.

Entertainment.headline: Robert Carradine, 'Revenge of the Nerds' and 'Lizzie McGuire' star, dies at 71
Entertainment.detail: Robert Carradine, known for his roles in 'Revenge of the Nerds' and 'Lizzie McGuire,' has passed away at the age of 71, prompting memories from Ever Carradine, Martha Plimpton, Hilary Duff and more.

Entertainment.headline: Virosh Wedding: Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda tied the knot today
Entertainment.detail: South Indian cinema actors, Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda, have officially tied the knot on February 26 at 8:00 am in an Andhra tradition, celebrated by fans with the name 'Virosh'.

Crime & Justice.headline: Brazil's Supreme Court imposes steep sentences for Marielle Franco murder
Crime & Justice.detail: Brazil's Supreme Court convicted five men for plotting the 2018 assassination of Marielle Franco, a human rights leader and politician, and her driver, Anderson Gomes, handing down lengthy prison sentences. The case raised questions about political violence, corruption, and race in Brazilian society.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man To Be Charged With Evasion Of A Police Roadblock, Dangerous Driving And Other Traffic Offences
Crime & Justice.detail: A 34-year-old man in Singapore will be charged in court for evading a police roadblock and dangerous driving after leading officers on an hour-long pursuit in a deregistered car with a cloned number plate and without a valid license. The incident occurred on February 17, 2026, and the man was arrested on February 24, 2026.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Dept checking if any Epstein-related records were mistakenly withheld
Crime & Justice.detail: The US Justice Department is reviewing whether it improperly withheld documents from the Jeffrey Epstein files, following reports that some records involving accusations against Donald Trump were missing from the released documents. The review was prompted by news reports that summaries of FBI interviews with a woman who accused both Trump and Epstein of sexual assault were not included in the released files.

Crime & Justice.headline: Sentencing hearing for teen who set girl on fire at Saskatoon high school
Crime & Justice.detail: A sentencing hearing is scheduled for a teenager who pleaded guilty to attempted murder after setting her former friend on fire at a Saskatoon high school in September 2024. The victim, who was 15 at the time, suffered severe burns after the attacker splashed lighter fluid on her and set her ablaze in a hallway.

Crime & Justice.headline: Waste trafficking driving toxic pollution and harming public health, says UNODC
Crime & Justice.detail: The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) reported that waste trafficking is causing toxic pollution and damaging public health. This announcement was made in a press release on February 25, 2026.
Crime & Justice.source: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

Education.headline: All mainstream schools 'have a role to play' to accommodate students with special educational needs
Education.detail: The Education Minister in Northern Ireland has stated that all mainstream schools will be required to facilitate students with special educational needs (SEN), acknowledging the increasing need for SEN provisions. This decision aims to ensure that children with SEN have access to appropriate specialist placements in their local communities.

Education.headline: Judge Blocks Texas Law Banning L.G.B.T.Q. Clubs in Some School Districts
Education.detail: A judge has blocked a Texas law that would have banned L.G.B.T.Q. clubs in some school districts. This ruling protects the rights of students to form and participate in these clubs.

Education.headline: Wisconsin schools, teachers file lawsuit against GOP-led Legislature seeking more funding
Education.detail: A coalition of Wisconsin school districts, teachers' unions, advocacy groups, parents, and students has filed a lawsuit against the state Legislature, alleging that it is failing to adequately fund public schools. The lawsuit aims to increase funding for public education in Wisconsin.

Education.headline: Lawrence Summers Will Resign From Harvard After Epstein Revelations
Education.detail: Lawrence Summers will resign from Harvard following revelations related to Jeffrey Epstein. This marks a significant development in the ongoing fallout from the release of documents related to Epstein's activities.

Education.headline: US childhood literacy rates are lagging. Pediatricians could be part of the solution
Education.detail: With US childhood literacy rates lagging, pediatricians are being considered as part of the solution, with hospitals starting to check literacy skills during pediatric visits for kids as young as 3. This initiative aims to address the declining literacy rates among children in the United States.

Lifestyle.headline: Word of the Day for February 26, 2026: sibug-sibug
Lifestyle.detail: ABS-CBN Lifestyle provides a daily Tagalog word.

Lifestyle.headline: Feng shui predictions ni Master Hanz Cua para sa Pebrero 26, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Get your daily dose of Feng Shui predictions from Master Hanz Cua.

Lifestyle.headline: Possible bullet holes mysteriously appear on American Airlines plane after flight from Colombia
Lifestyle.detail: An American Airlines plane is being inspected after what appeared to be bullet holes were found after a flight from Colombia.

Lifestyle.headline: Shoppers report saving $15-40 per trip with the viral 6-to-1 grocery method
Lifestyle.detail: Social media users say that using a structured shopping list cuts down on impulse buys and food waste.

Lifestyle.headline: London Fashion Week Fall/Winter 2026 trends to know.
Lifestyle.detail: Stay up to date with the latest trends from London Fashion Week Fall/Winter 2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Deadly crash at Reagan Airport highlights need for FAA reform
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A recent deadly crash at Reagan Airport underscores the urgent need for reforms within the FAA to improve safety and prevent future tragedies.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: England's migration awakening
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses the shifting perspectives and growing awareness in England regarding migration issues.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Government must realise that 7 shirtless Youth Congress protesters do not a national threat make
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An Indian Express editorial criticizing the government for overreacting to a minor protest, arguing it does not constitute a national security threat.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on violent online rhetoric: all politicians have a duty to set a civil tone
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Politicians have a responsibility to promote respectful dialogue in the face of rising violent online rhetoric, according to The Guardian's viewpoint.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on saving Westminster: parliament should leave London
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian argues that relocating parliament from London would be beneficial.

World.headline: USS Gerald Ford leaves Crete as Iran talks start: Report
World.detail: The world's largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, departed from a naval base in Crete as indirect talks between the U.S. and Iran commenced in Geneva, mediated by Oman's foreign minister. The vessel's departure follows a period of heightened military presence in the region amid ongoing tensions.

World.headline: Iran enters critical nuclear talks with US insisting deal is within reach
World.detail: Amid a massive buildup of US warships and aircraft in the Middle East, Iran and the US have commenced critical nuclear talks in Geneva. Oman is mediating the discussions.

World.headline: WHO European Region projected to remain worst globally by 2030 in tobacco crisis
World.detail: The WHO European Region, encompassing 53 countries in Europe and Central Asia, is projected to maintain the highest tobacco use prevalence globally by 2030, with concerning trends among women and young people. The region is unlikely to meet the global target of a 30% reduction in tobacco use among women by 2025 and has the highest e-cigarette use among adolescents.

World.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen calls March election amid 'Greenland bounce' in polls
World.detail: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen has called for an election in March, capitalizing on recent poll gains following her administration's firm stance against Donald Trump's efforts to control Greenland. The Social Democrats are polling at 22-23%, up from 18% in December.

World.headline: U.S. orders diplomats to fight data sovereignty initiatives
World.detail: The Trump administration has instructed U.S. diplomats to lobby against data sovereignty initiatives, viewing them as potentially disruptive to global data flows and AI-related services. This move signals a more confrontational approach towards countries seeking to regulate how U.S. tech companies handle data.

Zimbabwe.headline: Lithium prices soar after Zimbabwe suspends export
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has suspended the export of all raw minerals, leading to a surge in lithium prices. The government stated the move is in the national interest, citing malpractices and leakages.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZSE suspends trading in OK shares after corporate rescue deal
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE) has suspended trading in OK Zimbabwe shares following a corporate rescue deal. This comes after the retail giant faced massive losses and revenue collapse.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe rejects $350m US health deal, citing sovereignty concerns
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has declined a US health deal worth $350 million, expressing concerns over sovereignty. The government raised issues about conditions attached to the deal, particularly those related to sharing health data.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZANU PF Harare spokesperson wanted in South Africa after skipping bail
Zimbabwe.detail: Joachim Chivayo, a ZANU PF Harare spokesperson, is wanted in South Africa after fleeing while on bail in connection with an R15 million illegal gold dealing case.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe pulls out of US health aid talks
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has ended health assistance negotiations with the U.S. after disagreements over a proposed funding agreement, specifically a requirement to share sensitive health data.
